metadata.title: Context Hygiene: Prune Before You Paste
metadata.description_blurb: Pasting six documents when one pruned excerpt is needed doesn't make Claude smarter — it makes Claude weight the wrong source, and the output fails silently.

beats.narration_text: Irrelevant context does not just fail to help. It distorts. Claude weights context by structural prominence — a large formal document outweighs a recent email, even when the email is what matters. Pasting six documents when one pruned excerpt is needed does not make Claude smarter. It makes Claude weight the wrong source and fail silently. This is Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: Write a paragraph summarizing where we are. [3 status reports + project charter + 40-message email thread pasted]

beats.narration_text: Four context types. Essential: information that changes the output if absent. Useful: adds value but not required. Neutral: neither helps nor hurts. Harmful: actively degrades output by shifting weight to the wrong source. Most context dumps contain all four in unknown proportions. The pruning question is: which type is each piece?
beats.new_visual_element: Four context types — essential / useful / neutral / harmful

beats.narration_text: Harmful context: a large formal document that outweighs the source you actually need. The project charter is forty pages of approved scope from eight months ago. The recent email is two paragraphs from the stakeholder who changed the scope last week. Paste both unlabeled and Claude weights the charter — because it is bigger, more structured, and arrived first.
beats.new_visual_element: Harmful context — big formal doc outweighs recent email

beats.narration_text: Label before you paste. 'Source A [authoritative — use this for the current state].' 'Source B [background only — do not let this override Source A].' Unlabeled sources make Claude guess the authority ranking. Labeled sources give Claude the ranking you intend.
beats.new_visual_element: Label lines — authoritative vs background only

beats.narration_text: Declare exclusions explicitly. 'Ignore the appendices.' 'The attachments are background only — do not draw conclusions from them.' Exclusion declarations are constraints on context, not context itself. Without them, Claude uses what is there — even the parts that should not shape the output.
beats.new_visual_element: Exclusion declaration — explicit instruction to ignore

beats.narration_text: Two minutes of pruning before pasting. Identify essential context, label sources with authority rankings, declare exclusions, and paste only the excerpt the task draws on. Curated context outperforms comprehensive dumps — not because Claude is limited, but because weighting is a function of what is present, not what is relevant.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — pruned context block vs full dump comparison

beats.narration_text: Audit your context before your next paste. Use this prompt to label and prune.

props.command: Before I paste documents, help me audit them for context hygiene. For each source I name, classify it as: essential (output changes if absent), useful (adds value), neutral, or harmful (may distort weighting). Then tell me what to label as authoritative, what to label as background only, and what to exclude entirely.

beats.narration_text: If this stops the wrong document from winning, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
